mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.63% provides room for rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 0.5% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159001 thousand provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or correlation between PAYEMS changes and 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously fall below 1.3 million annualized units, indicating demand-side weakness that compresses equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1.3 million annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 757085 million and HOUST at 1465 thousand units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST deterioration produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) index falls below 102.0 (current level 102.6475) signaling supply-side contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.6475 is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO declines and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 4.0%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive, forcing the Fed into further tightening that compresses equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 333.979 and FEDFUNDS at 3.63%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (inflation-rate gap) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% from current 4.3%, signaling labor market deterioration that increases equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market softening in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE increases and 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously contract below 1.4 million units, indicating broad-based demand weakness that triggers equity multiple compression over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1.4 million
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24152.656 billion and HOUST at 1465 thousand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of GDP and housing weakness produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3.5% year-over-year, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY < 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.6475 and CPIAUCSL at 333.979
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (supply growth with price stability) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
